file_format: CSV (IHME GBD Results Tool export; comma-separated; UTF-8; header row; not tracked by git)
description_and_role: GBD 2023 RHD + all-cause epidemiology for Indonesia, 1990-2009 chunk. Supplies RHD incidence/prevalence/death rates (become disease-model rate arrays in 03) and RHD + all-cause counts (become calibration targets in 04/04b). Row-bound with the 2010-2023 chunk (epi_idn_2010_2023) via rbindlist in 01.
variables_indicators_used: measure_name (Deaths, Incidence, Prevalence); cause_name (Rheumatic heart disease, All causes); metric_name (Rate -> per-100k rates in 03; Number -> calibration target counts in 04/04b); sex_name; age_name; year; val; upper & lower (GBD 95% UI -> inverse-variance weights in 04); location_name (filter)
geographic_coverage: Indonesia: national + 34 subnational provinces (35 locations). Pipeline keeps only location_name=='Indonesia'; subnational rows are discarded.
age_coverage: 22 GBD age groups (<1 yr, 12-23 mo, 2-4 yr, then 5-yr bands to 95+)
unit_of_observation: One GBD estimate per location x year x sex x age-group x cause x measure x metric
source_institution: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME)
original_source_publication_url: GBD 2023, Global Burden of Disease Results Tool. Indonesia epidemiology permalink: https://collab2023.healthdata.org/gbd-results?params=gbd-api-2023-permalink/7630747596ef0f77cc0bd11bff01f241
consuming_scripts_functions: 01_prepare_inputs.R (list.files + data.table::fread + rbindlist; filter cause_name in dx_include and location_name==LOCATION; writes temp_baseline_rates_gbd.rds). Indirect via that RDS: 03 (rate_matrix), 04 (build_rhd_tps, grab_target), 04b (gbd_mat)
update_frequency_reference_version: GBD 2023 release; re-extract when IHME publishes a new GBD round (irregular, ~1-3 yr cycle)
processing_notes_assumptions_limitations: 01 globs EVERY .csv in data-raw/epidemiology/ indiscriminately, so only the intended country's chunks should sit in that folder. This is one of two Indonesia year-chunks (GBD download row-limit split); the split is invisible after rbindlist. Rate metric is per 100,000 (03 divides val by 1e5). Not tracked by git; must be re-downloaded.

relative_path: NA (R package 'wpp2024'; devtools::install_github('PPgp/wpp2024'); not stored in repo)
file_format: R lazy-loaded data object via data(popAge1dt)
description_and_role: UN WPP2024 OBSERVED single-year population estimates -- the observed population backbone (OBS_YEARS, default 1990-2024; rows <=2023 come from here, 2024 from popprojAge1dt). Spline-smoothed by single-year age and rescaled to preserve each sex-year total, then written to pop_observed.rds.
variables_indicators_used: name (country); year; age (0-100); popF, popM (thousands; multiplied by 1e3 to persons)
geographic_coverage: all UN countries/areas; filtered to COUNTRY (Indonesia or Uganda) via MODEL_COUNTRIES/ISO3
time_coverage: 1949-2023 per 02 header note (observed estimates); pipeline uses OBS_YEARS<=2023 (default from 1990)
unit_of_observation: Persons per country x year x single-year age x sex
source_institution: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division
original_source_publication_url: World Population Prospects 2024 (https://population.un.org/wpp/); R package: https://github.com/PPgp/wpp2024
consuming_scripts_functions: 02_build_demography.R (data(popAge1dt); pull_wpp_slice; smooth_age_distribution). Downstream pop_observed.rds -> 04 (build_rhd_tps, calibration denominators)
update_frequency_reference_version: WPP2024 revision (UN releases WPP roughly every 2 yr); pinned to the installed wpp2024 package version
processing_notes_assumptions_limitations: WPP reports thousands (x1e3 in pull_wpp_slice). The 2024 slice is taken from popprojAge1dt (WPP jump-off), not this table. Single-year ages are spline-smoothed to remove age-heaping (max/mean single-age shift is logged). No renv.lock in repo -> record the installed wpp2024 package version for reproducibility (needs confirmation).

relative_path: NA (R package 'wpp2024'; devtools::install_github('PPgp/wpp2024'); not stored in repo)
file_format: R lazy-loaded data object via data(popprojAge1dt)
description_and_role: UN WPP2024 medium-variant single-year population PROJECTION plus the 2024 jump-off/base year. Supplies (a) the 2024 last-observed slice appended to pop_observed.rds and (b) the projection backbone pop_projection.rds (PROJ_YEARS, default 2025 onward), taken directly with no smoothing (UN projections are already graduated).
variables_indicators_used: name (country); year (stored as character; coerced to integer in 02); age (0-100); popF, popM (thousands -> persons)
geographic_coverage: all UN countries/areas; filtered to COUNTRY (Indonesia or Uganda)
time_coverage: 2024-2100 (medium variant) per 02 header note; pipeline uses 2024 jump-off + PROJ_YEARS (default 2025..ANALYSIS_YEAR_END)
unit_of_observation: Persons per country x year x single-year age x sex
source_institution: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division
original_source_publication_url: World Population Prospects 2024 (https://population.un.org/wpp/); R package: https://github.com/PPgp/wpp2024
consuming_scripts_functions: 02_build_demography.R (data(popprojAge1dt); pull_wpp_slice). Downstream pop_projection.rds -> 03 (model horizon + base-year anchor), 05 (population array over horizon)
update_frequency_reference_version: WPP2024 revision (UN ~biennial); pinned to installed wpp2024 package version
processing_notes_assumptions_limitations: MEDIUM-VARIANT ONLY -- no high/low fertility or other variant sensitivity is drawn. 03 clips this projection to ANALYSIS_YEARS and errors if the analysis window exceeds the projection years. WPP thousands x1e3. year column is character in this table (02 coerces).

relative_path: data/<COUNTRY>/calibration_targets_stage.csv OR data-raw/calibration_targets_stage.csv (first existing path is used)
sheet_or_table_name: NA (single flat table; schema mirrors calibration_targets_stage_template.csv written by 04)
description_and_role: Local echocardiographic RHD stage-prevalence targets for the Layer-2 A/B/C/D structural stage calibration. If present, 04b adds a weighted squared-log-error stage-prevalence loss so the stage split is data-pinned. If ABSENT (current state), Layer-2 falls back to clinical priors + partial identification and 04 writes the empty schema template calibration_targets_stage_template.csv.
variables_indicators_used: Template schema (columns): location; year; sex; age_lo; age_hi; stage (A/B/C/D); target_prevalence; target_type; weight
geographic_coverage: Local / setting-specific (to be supplied; e.g. Indonesia or Uganda echo screening studies)
unit_of_observation: One stage-prevalence target per location x year x sex x age band x stage
source_institution: User-supplied (local echocardiographic screening studies); not an IHME/UN product
original_source_publication_url: NA (no external publication yet; provide the local echo study citation when the file is added)
consuming_scripts_functions: 04_calibration_random_tp.R (STAGE_TARGET_CANDIDATES; file.exists check; sets stage_calibration status), 04b_calibrate_structural.R (stage_target_file; adds stage-prevalence loss term when present)
update_frequency_reference_version: NA -- supply once local echo stage-prevalence data are available
processing_notes_assumptions_limitations: DOES NOT EXIST in the repo as of this writing, so the A/B/C/D stage split is prior-dominated / partially identified (see calibration_structural_identifiability.csv). Populate this file to pin stage prevalences. Path is COUNTRY-scoped under data/<COUNTRY>/; data-raw/ fallback also checked.

file_name: NA (embedded numeric vector abc_raw in R source; not a file)
file_format: Embedded R numeric vector (hard-coded constant; not a file)
description_and_role: External literature-derived relative prevalence split of living RHD across stages A/B/C. Normalised to the non-D share and combined with rhd_d_fraction to (03) split the prevalent RHD pool into A/B/C/D for seeding, and (04b) as the reference clinical split. Registered here because it is external DATA embedded in code, not a model assumption tuned internally.
geographic_coverage: Clinical cohort (multi-state RHD model); NOT Indonesia/Uganda-specific; applied uniformly to all ages/sexes/years
age_coverage: Applied uniformly across all ages (not age-specific)
time_coverage: Static (single published estimate; no time dimension)
unit_of_observation: Stage-prevalence proportion (dimensionless, sums to ~1 across A/B/C)
source_institution: Cannon J. et al (academic; multi-state RHD progression model, n=591)
original_source_publication_url: See docs/nihms-1800209.pdf in this repo; exact citation/DOI/URL NOT stated in code -- needs manual confirmation
consuming_scripts_functions: 03_build_disease_model.R (stage_split construction); 04b_calibrate_structural.R (abc/stage_split_of, burn-in reference). Inherited by 05 seed when 04b calibrated per-age shares are unavailable
update_frequency_reference_version: Static literature value; revisit if a better/local multi-state estimate is published
processing_notes_assumptions_limitations: Not local data -- a single external clinical estimate applied uniformly, so the A/B/C base split is an assumption, not a country calibration. Stage-D share (rhd_d_fraction, prior 0.10) is a separate [CALIBRATE] parameter derived as the equilibrium D-share by 04b. Confirm the exact Cannon et al citation and cohort applicability.

file_name: NA (embedded numeric scalar gdp_pc_base in R source; not a file)
relative_path: scripts/00_run_all.R (lines ~480-485; block J); consumed by scripts/08_economic_evaluation.R
file_format: Embedded R numeric scalar per COUNTRY (hard-coded constant; not a file)
description_and_role: External economic data value: base-year GDP per capita (US$) used only in 08 to monetise averted RHD deaths -- value of a statistical life (VSL = vsl_mult x GDP-pc) plus a GDP-pc productivity term, grown at gdp_growth from the base year across the analysis horizon.
variables_indicators_used: GDP per capita, current US$, base year 2019 (per country)
time_coverage: 2019 base year, grown to each analysis year at gdp_growth (3.0-3.2%/yr)
unit_of_observation: GDP per capita (US$) per country, base year
source_institution: World Bank (World Development Indicators) per 00_run_all.R comment
original_source_publication_url: https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/NY.GDP.PCAP.CD -- exact series/vintage NOT cited in code; needs manual confirmation
update_frequency_reference_version: World Bank WDI updated annually; base year 2019 fixed in code
processing_notes_assumptions_limitations: LIMITATION: the values used (4150; 1340) differ from the World Bank figures cited in the SAME code comment (~4107 Indonesia 2019; ~822 Uganda 2019) -- reconcile the exact WB series/year/value. Only block-J monetary values are registered here; the other economic parameters (disc_rate 3%, vsl_mult 30x, dalys_per_death 30, unit costs cost_screen/cost_sap/cost_surgery) are [PAPER]/[LIT]/[CALIBRATE] modelling ASSUMPTIONS, not tabulated raw data -- they belong in a separate parameter-provenance table.
